---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Sigmoid Commercial Intelligence Lab"
toc: false
anchor-sections: false
code-tools: false
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-eyebrow}
<span class="chip chip-grade-c no-dot">v0.1 · Hypothesis stage</span>
<span>A commercial decision system, not a documentation site</span>
:::

# Sigmoid Commercial Intelligence Lab {.hero-title}

::: {.hero-sub}
Commercial strategy as an evidence-based decision system: three strategic decisions, the evidence
behind them, the frameworks that score them, and the dashboards that bring them to life — all honest
about how much is still unproven.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[Start here](start-here/index.qmd){.btn-cil .btn-cil-primary}
[View the decisions](decisions/index.qmd){.btn-cil .btn-cil-ghost}
[Open dashboards](dashboards/index.qmd){.btn-cil .btn-cil-ghost}
[Read the CIS](cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md){.btn-cil .btn-cil-ghost}
:::

::: {.hero-meta}
<span>Governed by the Commercial Intelligence Specification</span>
<span>Synthetic data — nothing validated yet</span>
:::
:::

## How this lab works

::: {.section-lead}
The whole site is one workflow. You start from a **decision**, weigh the **evidence**, score it with a
**framework**, explore it in a **dashboard**, and only then — when evidence is strong enough — reach a
**recommendation**. New to it? [**Start here**](start-here/index.qmd).
:::

::: {.lifecycle}
<li><span class="stage"><span class="num">1</span>Decisions</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage"><span class="num">2</span>Evidence</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage"><span class="num">3</span>Frameworks</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage"><span class="num">4</span>Dashboards</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage is-loop"><span class="num">5</span>Recommendations</span></li>
:::

## The three decisions {.mt-section}

::: {.section-lead}
The lab exists to answer three questions. Everything else supports them.
:::

::: {.cil-cards}
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Decision 1 · Market entry</div>
<div class="card-title">Which segment should Sigmoid target first?</div>
<p>Seven candidate segments, scored on a transparent weighted framework.</p>
<span class="card-go">Open decision</span>
[](decisions/market-entry.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Market Entry decision"}
:::
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Decision 2 · Positioning</div>
<div class="card-title">CRM, Revenue Operations, or AI Readiness Platform?</div>
<p>Three framings compared across six weighted criteria — evidence strength included.</p>
<span class="card-go">Open decision</span>
[](decisions/positioning.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Positioning decision"}
:::
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Decision 3 · GTM gate</div>
<div class="card-title">What evidence is required before committing to GTM?</div>
<p>A confidence gate that keeps an unvalidated thesis from being scaled prematurely.</p>
<span class="card-go">Open decision</span>
[](decisions/gtm-evidence-gate.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open GTM Evidence Gate decision"}
:::
:::

## Choose your path {.mt-section}

::: {.section-lead}
Three ways in, depending on why you came.
:::

::: {.cil-cards}
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Investor / Operator</div>
<h3>See the thesis under test</h3>
<p>The pitch, the decisions in play, and where the evidence stands today.</p>
::: {.path-route}
<span class="step is-start">Product Brief</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Decisions</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Dashboards</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Status</span>
:::
<span class="card-go">Read the Product Brief</span>
[](start-here/product-brief.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Investor path"}
:::
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Product / Commercial</div>
<h3>Work the decisions</h3>
<p>Open the decisions, tune the framework weights, track what would move each one.</p>
::: {.path-route}
<span class="step is-start">Decisions</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Frameworks</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Dashboards</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Evidence</span>
:::
<span class="card-go">Open the Decisions</span>
[](decisions/index.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Product path"}
:::
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Research / Data Science</div>
<h3>Inspect the machinery</h3>
<p>The governing standard, the evidence model, the frameworks, and the tests that guard them.</p>
::: {.path-route}
<span class="step is-start">CIS</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Evidence</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Frameworks</span><span class="arw">→</span><span class="step">Tests</span>
:::
<span class="card-go">Read the CIS</span>
[](cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md){.stretched-link aria-label="Research path"}
:::
:::

## Why the proposal is Hypothesis v0.1 {.mt-section}

::: {.section-lead}
The source proposal is a persuasive argument made *before* the evidence is in. Treating it as truth
would bake in untested assumptions.
:::

So it enters the lab as **Hypothesis v0.1**: a set of falsifiable claims, each registered and
scheduled for validation. Proposal-sourced claims carry low quality and high bias — they can *raise* a
hypothesis, never *validate* one. See exactly which claims rest on the proposal under
[Evidence › Hypothesis v0.1](evidence/proposal-hypothesis-v01.qmd).

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
**What this means in practice.** Every score, ranking, and figure on this site is computed from
*synthetic* sample data and carries confidence grade **C or D**. Nothing is presented as a validated
market fact. See the live picture on [Current Status](start-here/current-status.qmd).
:::
